---
title: "Butterweed (Packera glabella)"
slug: "packera-glabella"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475151420/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Butterweed"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Asterales"
tax_family: "Asteraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Asterales", "Asteraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475151420/medium.jpg" alt="Butterweed" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/damien6868">
damien6868</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264604864">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Asterales
-   **Family:** Asteraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=54921)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats.

## Natural history notes

A spring-blooming wildflower characterized by its smooth stems, deeply lobed leaves, and clusters of bright yellow, daisy-like flowers. In Florida, it is commonly found in moist to wet habitats such as floodplains, ditches, and disturbed areas. It grows primarily during the cooler months and often appears as an early-season annual, providing nectar for pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/54921-Packera-glabella)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-packera-glabella/)
